info.title: LinkedIn Agentic AI Platform
info.description:
## LinkedIn Agentic AI Platform API

A distributed LinkedIn-style platform with microservices, Kafka event streaming,
Redis caching, and Agentic AI workflows.

### Services
- **Profile Service** — Member CRUD and search (`/members/*`)
- **Recruiter Service** — Recruiter management (`/recruiters/*`)
- **Job Service** — Job postings CRUD, search, and save (`/jobs/*`)
- **Application Service** — Job applications and status management (`/applications/*`)
- **Messaging Service** — Threads and messages (`/threads/*`, `/messages/*`)
- **Connection Service** — Connection requests and management (`/connections/*`)
- **Analytics Service** — Events, dashboards, and metrics (`/events/*`, `/analytics/*`)
- **AI Agent Service** — Agentic AI workflows with Ollama (`/ai/*`)

### Architecture
- **MySQL** — Transactional data (profiles, jobs, applications)
- **MongoDB** — Event logs, agent traces, unstructured data
- **Redis** — SQL query caching and session management
- **Kafka** — Async event streaming and agent orchestration
- **Ollama** — Local LLM for AI agent skills

post.description:
Create a new member profile with all attributes.
Returns an error if the email already exists (duplicate prevention).

Retrieve a member's full profile by member_id.
Uses Redis caching for frequently accessed profiles.

Update specific fields of a member's profile.
Only non-null fields in the request will be updated.
Permanently delete a member profile and all associated data.

Search members by keyword (name, headline, about), skill, or location.
Results are paginated and cached for performance.
Create a new recruiter/employer admin account.
Retrieve a recruiter's profile by recruiter_id.
Update specific fields of a recruiter's profile.
Permanently delete a recruiter account.

Create a new job posting. The recruiter_id must reference an existing recruiter.
Publishes a job.created event to Kafka.
Retrieve a job posting's full details by job_id. Publishes a job.viewed event.
Update specific fields of a job posting.

Search job postings by keyword, location, type, work mode, seniority, and skills.
Results are paginated and cached for 60 seconds.
Close a job posting (open → closed). Applications to closed jobs will be rejected.
List all job postings created by a specific recruiter.
Save a job posting to a member's saved list.

Submit an application to a job posting.
Handles: duplicate application, closed job, and missing member/job errors.
Retrieve an application's full details by application_id.
List all applications for a specific job posting (recruiter view).
List all applications submitted by a specific member.

Update the status of an application.
Valid statuses: submitted, reviewing, rejected, interview, offer.
Add a recruiter note or decision rationale to an application.

Create a new messaging thread between participants.
Each participant is identified by user_id and user_type (member/recruiter).
Retrieve thread metadata and participant list.
List all messaging threads for a specific user.

Send a message in a thread. Includes retry logic for failure handling.
Publishes a message.sent event to Kafka.
List all messages in a thread, ordered by timestamp (newest first).

Send a connection request from one member to another.
Handles: self-connection, duplicate request, and already connected errors.
Accept a pending connection request. Updates both members' connection counts.
Reject a pending connection request.
List all accepted connections for a member.
Find mutual connections between two members (extra credit).

Ingest a tracking event from UI or services.
Events are stored in MongoDB and published to Kafka for async processing.

Get top job postings by metric (applications, views, or saves).
Used for recruiter dashboard analytics.

Get the view → save → apply funnel for a specific job posting.
Data sourced from MongoDB event logs and MySQL records.
Get the city/state distribution of applicants for a specific job posting.
Get member dashboard metrics: profile views (last 30 days) and application status breakdown.

Start the Hiring Assistant multi-step AI workflow:
1. Parse resumes for all candidates
2. Match candidates against job requirements
3. Rank and shortlist top N candidates
4. Generate personalized outreach drafts

Returns a task_id to track progress via /ai/task-status or WebSocket.
Check the current status and progress of an AI task.

Human-in-the-loop: approve or reject the AI-generated shortlist and outreach drafts.
The recruiter must review the AI output before any action is taken.

Standalone resume parsing endpoint. Uses Ollama LLM with regex fallback.
Extracts: skills, experience, education, contact info, summary.

post.summary: Match a candidate to a job (standalone skill)
post.description:
Standalone job-candidate matching endpoint.
Computes match score based on skills overlap (50%), location (20%), seniority (30%).
List all active and recent AI tasks.

8+ years of experience in Python, Java, and cloud technologies. Led a team of 5 engineers at Google working on distributed systems using Kubernetes and AWS.
